hypothesis: If a cross-modal model aligns graph, text, and XRD latent representations by training two modalities to predict the third with VIC-regularized contrastive losses, then it will achieve lower MAE on Matbench crystal property tasks and AFLOW band gap prediction than single-modality baselines (CGCNN/CoGN/ALIGNN), because integrating complementary modalities produces richer, more generalizable material representations even with minimal finetuning.
